LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF TO APPEAR AT DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER ON A DEPOSITION DATE FOR MS. GUERRERO TO TAKE PLACE NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 13, 2013. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $4,000.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, NA AND AGAINST MS. GUERRERO TO BE PAID WITHIN 60 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/EHG)

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A'S Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Appear At Deposition and Request for Monetary Sanction is Granted. No opposition filed. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Christina Goebelsmann of Wargo + French LLp (30853-6807) via CourtCall appearance for defendant JPMorgan Chase moving party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Speculative damages/loss of money allegedly resulting from violation of CCC 2923.5 (SAC 67) cannot satisfy standing requirement for alleged violation of Penal Code 115. Plaintiff's opposition fails to address lack of particularity in 75-78 per Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal. App. 612; and privilege per CCC 47 and CCC 2924(d)(2). Prevailing party prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)
